meta.title: Heimlich Maneuver
notes: Universal sign of choking (hands to throat), inability to talk, cough, or breathe, panicked expression, cyanosis.
Standing victim: support the patient (they may faint); stand firmly on your feet.
As a first responder in an ambulance, it is highly likely that upon your arrival, the patient will be unconscious.
Emphasizing the need for medical assessment.

chk_01.text: Suspect foreign body obstruction? (Universal sign: hands to throat, inability to talk, cough, or breathe, panicked expression, cyanosis)

chk_04.text: Is the patient conscious or unconscious?

chk_06.text: Adult / Child: 5 back blows, then 5 abdominal thrusts.

chk_07.text: Infant / Pregnant / Obese: 5 back blows, then 5 chest thrusts. [Infant: face-down on your forearm with the head lower than the body for the 5 back blows, then turn them face-up and give 5 chest thrusts with two fingers below the nipple line.]

chk_08.text: Repeat the cycles until airway is cleared or patient becomes unconscious.

chk_12.text: CPR algorithm. (Choking-specific transition: after each set of chest compressions, check in the mouth for the foreign body.)

chk_13.text: No foreign-body obstruction suspected — return to General Intervention Process & Patient Assessment.
